title: Rough isometric-hold reference bands (dead hang, plank)
description: Baked ROUGH population-reference band ladders (weak/fair/good/excellent) for the dead-hang and plank hold tests (issue #1135). DISCLOSED-ROUGH by design: these holds have no standardized percentile norms, so a coarse cited band ladder — NOT a fabricated 1–99 percentile — colors the #1132 grid tiles by favorability while every surface renders the `quality: rough` disclosure. Sex-keyed only (the cited figures don't support age banding). Regenerate with `npm run gen:fitness-hold-norms`.

citation.source: Curated rough coaching/clinical rules of thumb for plank and dead-hang holds (McGill, Low Back Disorders; Bianco 2015; Strand 2014; Bohannon 2019 for grip as a marker). Coarse, non-standardized — a DISCLOSED ROUGH GUIDE.
citation.note: No standardized percentile norms exist for these holds; every threshold is a coarse, human-reviewable cutoff carrying an explicit `quality: rough` flag. INFORMATIONAL, not measurements or medical/coaching advice.

entries.name: deadhang
entries.source: Rough grip-endurance rules of thumb: ~30s baseline, ~60s good, ~120s excellent. Grip STRENGTH (not hang time) is the mortality-linked measure (Bohannon 2019); hang-time bands are a disclosed rough proxy, not a risk stratifier.

entries.name: plank
entries.source: Rough coaching/clinical rules of thumb: ~30s minimum, ~60s solid, ~120s strong (McGill trunk-endurance holds; Bianco 2015; Strand 2014). Coarse and non-standardized — a disclosed rough guide, not a validated norm.
